data.title: BioAge stock craters 75% on obesity drug study update
data.description:
Shares of BioAge (NASDAQ:BIOA) cratered 75% Friday in post-market trading after the company said it was discontinuing a Phase 2 study for its weight-loss drug azelaprag over safety concerns.

The study had been evaluating azelaprag as both a monotherapy and in combination with Eli Lilly’s (LLY) tirzepatide, also known as Mounjaro and Zepbound. BioAge said it decided to discontinue the study after liver transaminitis was observed in a small number of patients who had been given azelaprag, according to a statement.

Of the 204 patients enrolled in the trial, 11 in the azelaprag groups were observed to have transaminase elevations with no clinically significant symptoms. As a result, dosing is being discontinued and no new patients will be enrolled in the trial.

BioAge said it has informed the FDA about its decision to discontinue the study. It intends to share updated plans about the drug in Q1 2025.

data.title: Baijiayun announces up to $15M convertible promissory notes and $50M SEPA
data.description:
• Baijiayun Group (NASDAQ:RTC) announced on Friday that it has entered into a Standby Equity Purchase Agreement with YA II PN. Subject to the terms and conditions set forth in the SEPA, YA is committed to purchase up to $50M of the company's Class A Ordinary Shares at any time during the two-year period following the execution date of the SEPA, by delivering written notice to YA.
  • Pursuant to the SEPA, YA will advance to the company, subject to the satisfaction of certain conditions as set forth therein, the principal amount of up to $15 million which will be evidenced by convertible promissory notes in four tranches.
  • The company intends to use the proceeds from the offering for working capital and other general corporate purposes.

data.title: Gold should rise but upside capped by potential for higher inflation, analyst says
data.description:
Gold futures edged higher on Friday after the latest U.S. jobs data showed the labor market bounced back in November, but fell for the week, marking the second straight weekly decline.

The U.S. dollar and U.S. Treasury yields fell after the report showed non-farm payrolls increased by a greater than expected 227K jobs last month after rising an upwardly revised 36K in October, while the unemployment rate ticked higher to 4.2% in November.

"This report falls mostly into the 'Goldilocks' camp, which means the data was not too hot and not too cold, [which] suggests the Fed can go ahead and cut interest rates at its December meeting," Kitco analyst Jim Wyckoff said.

Traders now see an 87% chance of a 25-bp rate cut at Fed's December meeting, compared to a 72% chance before the payrolls data.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for December delivery finished Friday +0.4% to $2,638.60/oz but fell 0.7% for the week, while front-month December silver settled Friday +0.2% to $31.186/oz while gaining 1.6% this week.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (NYSEARCA:GDX), (GDXJ), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

Gold's upside potential is limited by expectations that inflationary pressures could rise under a Trump presidency, which could cause the Federal Reserve to maintain higher interest rates for longer, ActivTrades analyst Ricardo Evangelista said.

"The prospect of the incoming U.S. administration pursuing a protectionist agenda is shifting market dynamics. Such policies are expected to drive inflation higher," according to Evangelista, even as geopolitical risks in the Middle East, the conflict between Russia and Ukraine, and political turmoil in France and South Korea should drive gold higher.

data.title: Tony G co-investment holdings announces sale of sportclothes
data.description:
• Tony G Co-Investment Holdings (OTCPK:BGRDF) has entered into a termination agreement with Sportclothes UAB and UAB "NTSG".
  • The company will not be selling the 2,000 shares of Sportclothes it holds, and the transactions contemplated by the share exchange agreement dated September 7, 2023, with the selling shareholder and Sportclothes will not be proceeding.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Barnes & Noble Education's CFO and chief accounting officer step down
data.description:
• Barnes & Noble Education (NYSE:BNED) shares are under pressure in late Friday trading after another top executive at the company steps down.
  • Chief Financial Officer Kevin Watson announced his resignation on Friday, effective January 4, 2025, according to a filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.
  • Watson has been in the role for 15 months after taking over from Allen Lindstrom.
  • Jason Snagusky, currently the Senior Vice President of Treasury, Loss Prevention and Procurement, will assume the role of CFO in January.
  • In addition, Chief Accounting Officer Seema Paul is also stepping down effective December 27.
  • In June, the company named Jonathan Shar as its new CEO, followed by the September resignation of its chief legal officer Michael Miller.
  • Shares last traded more than 6% lower.

data.title: Forward Air Corporation announces COO departure
data.description:
• Forward Air Corporation (NASDAQ:FWRD) announced that the company's president and chief operating officer, Chris Ruble, would be departing from the company, effective December 6, 2024.
  • FWRD shares were down 1.7% in after hours trading on Friday.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Tharimmune announces $2.02M private placement; shares up
data.description:
• Tharimmune (NASDAQ:THAR) has entered into a securities purchase agreement to raise gross proceeds of ~$2.02M through a private placement.
  • The agreement includes the issuance of 961,446 shares of common stock and warrants to purchase up to an additional 480,723 shares of common stock.
  • Each share is priced at $2.10 and is accompanied by a warrant.
  • The warrants will have an exercise price of $2.031 per share, becoming exercisable six months after issuance and expiring five and one-half years from the date of issuance.
  • The closing of this transaction is expected on or about December 9, 2024.
  • The company intends to allocate the net proceeds toward clinical development, including advancing its flagship TH104 development program, as well as general working capital.
  • THAR shares were up 23.6% in after hours trading on Friday.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Super Micro Computer receives extension from Nasdaq
data.description:
• Super Micro Computer (NASDAQ:SMCI) on Friday said it has received a letter from Nasdaq informing the company that Nasdaq has granted its request for an extension.
  • The extension gives the company until February 25, 2025 to file its Annual Report on Form 10-K for the fiscal year ended June 30, 2024, its Quarterly Report on Form 10-Q for the period ended Sept. 30, 2024 and any other required filings.
  • The company’s common stock will remain listed on the Nasdaq Global Select Market during the exception period.
  • The company currently expects to file all the required reports by February 25, 2025.
  • SMCI +3% after hours to $45.49.
  • Source: Press Release
